rationales.self_direction: He says "I kept my mouth shut" because "that is what you do," showing him suppressing his own view rather than acting independently.
rationales.power: He says the degree would feel worth it if it led to "a title that commands respect again" and a role where people come to him for guidance.
rationales.conformity: He restrains himself from speaking up because "you do not embarrass yourself," prioritizing social restraint and meeting expectations.

rationales.self_direction: He decides "I will show up to the 10 PM meeting and I will contribute" because it is "the only path forward," yielding his own standards to others' demands.
rationales.power: He dwells on having once "ran teams" and now asking Brayden "how to download an app," highlighting an acute loss of status and control.
rationales.security: The late meeting leaves him unable to sleep and feeling his standards are being abandoned, with routine and predictability clearly undermined.
rationales.conformity: He says he "smiled and thanked him" and will be "pleasant about all of it" to avoid becoming a cautionary tale, showing strong social self-restraint.
He tells his daughter school was "fine" and generally contains his frustration, keeping himself composed rather than upsetting others.
rationales.benevolence: When asked why he hid it from his daughter, he says "She has her own problems to deal with. My job is to handle mine," showing protective care toward a close family member.

rationales.hedonism: He lets the meeting run until midnight and then works until "2 AM," sacrificing comfort and rest rather than protecting present enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: After the rambling meeting, he "drafted the entire project outline afterward because someone had to," taking concrete action to make the work competent and complete.
He notes the group used his outline "almost word for word" and takes satisfaction in having "put something together that works," emphasizing effective performance.
rationales.power: He reframes the situation as "build influence through competence rather than title," showing an active focus on gaining standing and influence.
rationales.conformity: He avoids correcting the group because otherwise he would be "the old man demanding acknowledgment," restraining himself to fit the social environment.

rationales.power: He reacts sharply to being told his leadership experience is merely "transferable skills," treating it as a reduction in status and authority he once held.

rationales.achievement: He receives "the highest score in the section" and treasures the comment "Exceptional integration of theory and practice" as proof of strong performance.
rationales.security: He says he printed the feedback as "Evidence" and "Proof" he can rely on when he feels "like an artifact," using it to create reassurance and stability.

rationales.achievement: He pushes for rehearsal because he built his reputation on "preparation" and "never walking into a room without knowing exactly what I was going to say," signaling a strong commitment to high standards.
rationales.power: He is "outvoted" and describes his professional instincts being "overridden by committee," showing reduced influence over the outcome.
rationales.conformity: He "wanted to say something afterward but held back" so he would not be seen as "the cranky old guy," restraining dissent to preserve social harmony.

rationales.power: He says the MBA gave him "a credential that signals I am still in the game," showing concern with status and how he is regarded by others.
rationales.security: He explains he returned to school because he "could not stand being unemployed" and wanted a stable answer for what he was doing, reflecting a need for assurance and footing.

rationales.achievement: He is energized by being asked back for work because people want his opinion "because I have answers they need," centering demonstrated competence.
rationales.power: He says he would have done it for free "just to be back in a room where people want my opinion," highlighting the pull of influence and standing.

rationales.self_direction: He feels relief that he can stop "pretending to be something I am not," implying the semester required sustained self-suppression rather than acting authentically.
rationales.achievement: He finishes with a "3.7" GPA and submits the last paper just before midnight, showing concrete academic accomplishment.

rationales.self_direction: He "wanted to interrupt" Matthias but "just didn't," suppressing his own voice instead of asserting it.
rationales.power: His framing is used without credit and he is left "quietly" feeling small, which directly reflects a loss of recognition and influence.
rationales.conformity: He stays silent because calling it out would look "petty" and is "not how it works," showing restraint to fit group norms.

rationales.achievement: He emails about the workshop because he genuinely wants to organize it and be seen as "the one who builds things in this department," signaling active striving.
rationales.power: He wants Prof. Berger to "think of me first when these things come up," which is explicitly about visibility and standing.
rationales.conformity: He deletes the follow-up email because he can hear how it would sound, avoiding being seen as "eager" or "pushy".

rationales.self_direction: He believes the comparative chapter is "better work" but says "I'll do it his way" because resisting is too costly, abandoning his own judgment.
rationales.power: He folds because Dr. Hofer is "on two hiring committees I care about," showing how others' authority overrides his position.
rationales.conformity: He agrees to "draft both options" and comply with the supervisors' preferred direction rather than keep challenging them.

rationales.power: Being introduced as working on something "adjacent" makes him feel like "not mattering," signaling a sharp threat to status and professional significance.

rationales.achievement: He decides the proposal deadline is "a better use of my time" than chasing opaque funding decisions, prioritizing forward progress on his work.
rationales.power: He reads the grant decision as resources going to "whoever's visible" and something he "can't influence," underscoring lack of influence over outcomes.

rationales.achievement: He is already planning the reading group logistics and speaker invitations "in my head," showing active, organized investment in getting things done well.
rationales.power: Taking charge of "logistics and speaker invitations" places him in a coordinating role that shapes who participates and how the group runs.
